tasks_completed.description: Changed PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS from 180 to 600 in agents/push-analyst.md Phase 7 Python snippet
tasks_completed.changes: Constant value changed from 180 to 600; adjacent 2-line comment replaced with 4-line comment describing 600s round-trip rationale and citing task dev-20260527-063758-T3
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: TTL 180s insufficient for orchestrator->push-analyst->orchestrator result-processing->execute-push.py round trip which frequently exceeds 180s

tasks_completed.description: Updated test_final_sweep.sh AC4 V1/V2/V3 assertions from 180 to 600
tasks_completed.changes: Line 64: grep pattern =180 -> =600; line 73: val.value==180 -> val.value==600; line 86: TTL.*180 pattern -> TTL.*600 (also strengthened to require 600 in name match)
tasks_completed.rationale: Regression assertions hard-coded 180; after constant change they would produce false FAIL; updated to assert 600

tasks_completed.description: Updated commands/push.md Push-analyst grant TTL section to document 600s
tasks_completed.changes: PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180 -> = 600; 180s prose -> 600s; 'raised from 120 to 180' backstory retained and extended with 'then raised from 180s to 600s in task dev-20260527-063758-T3'; removed 'The 180s TTL is the named constant' stale phrase
tasks_completed.rationale: Operational spec must document current TTL value; stale 180 references would be misleading

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — TTL value set by prior task 20260519-211515 R4 / AC4
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: TTL was previously raised from 120s to 180s to absorb subagent dispatch latency; that increment proved insufficient for slow/cold-path round trips exceeding 180s
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Increases TTL to 600s (10 minutes) to reliably cover the full orchestrator->push-analyst->orchestrator result-processing->execute-push.py round trip

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Lines added (17) exceed estimate (5) because comment rewording in push-analyst.md expanded from 2 lines to 4, and commands/push.md prose restructuring added context lines. All additions are documentation updates required by AC4; no logic lines added.

dev.qa_notes: AC1: grep agents/push-analyst.md for 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600' (expect 1 match). AC2: diff touches exactly 3 files (baseline-dirty files excluded from scope). AC3: verify lines 64, 73, 86 of test_final_sweep.sh reference 600 (not 180). AC4: grep commands/push.md for 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180' (expect 0 matches) and 'PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600' (expect 1 match).

self_verification.notes: AC1 verified: 1 match for PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600 in agents/push-analyst.md, 0 matches for = 180. AC3 verified: lines 64/73/86 of test_final_sweep.sh all reference 600. AC4 verified: commands/push.md has PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 600, no stale 180 assignment or '180s TTL is the named constant' prose. AC2 verified: git diff --name-only against baseline SHA shows exactly 3 target files changed; other modified files in working tree predate this task (baseline_dirty_snapshot).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex CLI returned only startup/init output (12,583 tokens used, 'Ready' acknowledgement) with no substantive findings. The exec command processed the prompt but produced no CODEX_FEEDBACK content — treated as no-findings result for this MICRO-tier constant change.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted: (1) no over-engineering — exactly 5 in-place substitutions; (2) no under-engineering — all 3 sites updated atomically; (3) no missed edge cases — AC4 V3 grep strengthened to require 600 in name match; (4) no regression risk — confirmed no stale 180 in assertions; (5) no scope drift — diff limited to 3 specified files

recommendations: If round-trip latency continues to grow, consider emitting a structured log entry when execute-push.py observes a near-expiry grant so the TTL can be tuned proactively
